mission.title: operator-framework: Predictable Release Names with the Helm Operator SDK
mission.description: Predictable Release Names with the Helm Operator SDK. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current operator-framework deployment
steps.description:
Verify your operator-framework version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
helm list -n operator-framework 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working operator-framework installation.

steps.title: Review operator-framework configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant operator-framework configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get configmap -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=operator-framework
```
## Type of question

General context and help around the operator-sdk.

## Question

**What did you do?**

Deployed a Helm Operator for a chart and then created a `CustomResource` for it.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Predictable Release Names with the Helm Operator SDK
steps.description:
I remembered we had some discussion about the release name in https://github.com/operator-framework/helm-app-operator-kit/issues/49 and https://github.com/operator-framework/helm-app-operator-kit/pull/57 before we integrated the Helm support into the SDK, so check that out for background about how
```yaml
oc new-project gitlab

oc adm policy add-scc-to-user anyuid -z default
oc adm policy add-scc-to-user anyuid -z gitlab-shared-secrets

oc create secret tls apps-ocplab-com --cert=$TLS_PATH/fullchain.pem --key=$TLS_PATH/privkey.pem

helm upgrade --install gitlab gitlab/gitlab \
  --timeout 600 \
  --set global.hosts.domain=apps.ocplab.com \
  --set global.hosts.externalIP=10.10.10.10 \
  --set certmanager-issuer.email=gnunn@redhat.com \
  --set nginx-ingress.enabled=false \
  --set prometheus.install=false \
  --set certmanager.install=false \
  --set global.ingress.configureCertmanager=false \

```

steps.title: Upgrade operator-framework to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade operator-framework:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade operator-framework operator-framework/operator-framework --namespace operator-framework
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework
helm list -n operator-framework
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get events -n operator-framework --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Predictable Release Names with the Helm Operator SDK" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I remembered we had some discussion about the release name in https://github.com/operator-framework/helm-app-operator-kit/issues/49 and https://github.com/operator-framework/helm-app-operator-kit/pull/57 before we integrated the Helm support into the SDK, so check that out for background about how we ended up with the current release name.
codeSnippets:
oc new-project gitlab

oc adm policy add-scc-to-user anyuid -z default
oc adm policy add-scc-to-user anyuid -z gitlab-shared-secrets

oc create secret tls apps-ocplab-com --cert=$TLS_PATH/fullchain.pem --key=$TLS_PATH/privkey.pem

helm upgrade --install gitlab gitlab/gitlab \
  --timeout 600 \
  --set global.hosts.domain=apps.ocplab.com \
  --set global.hosts.externalIP=10.10.10.10 \
  --set certmanager-issuer.email=gnunn@redhat.com \
  --set nginx-ingress.enabled=false \
  --set prometheus.install=false \
  --set certmanager.install=false \
  --set global.ingress.configureCertmanager=false \
  --set global.ingress.tls.secretName=apps-ocplab-com

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with operator-framework installed or the issue environment reproducible.
